title: The Sweetest Apu

jokes.text: Some special time. I get to stand around watching you sell fatty poisons to overfed Americans.
jokes.setup: Apu calls their work time together 'special time'
jokes.punchline: Manjula sarcastically describes it as watching him sell junk food to fat Americans
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural observation delivered with perfect character voice - Manjula's educated disdain for American consumer culture

jokes.text: You'd think that would deter me, but no.
jokes.setup: Manjula criticizes his job selling unhealthy food
jokes.punchline: Apu acknowledges the criticism doesn't affect him at all
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery, economical phrasing, shows Apu's resignation to his situation

jokes.text: The porno magazine buyers are too embarrassed to make their move.
jokes.setup: Children are present in the store
jokes.punchline: This is preventing adult customers from buying pornography
jokes.explanation: Clever situational comedy - children inadvertently cockblocking porn sales

jokes.text: Cleanup in all the aisles.
jokes.setup: Normal 'cleanup in aisle X' announcement
jokes.punchline: ALL aisles need cleanup - suggests total chaos from the octuplets
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - takes a familiar retail phrase and escalates it to absurdity with just two words changed

jokes.text: In a Civil War reenactment, we need lots of Indians to shoot.
jokes.setup: Customer inviting Apu to Civil War reenactment
jokes.punchline: Casually racist assumption that they need 'Indians to shoot'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about casual American racism - historically accurate ignorance about Native Americans vs. Indian immigrants

jokes.text: I don't know which part of that sentence to correct first.
jokes.setup: Customer's historically and culturally ignorant invitation
jokes.punchline: Apu's overwhelmed reaction to multiple layers of wrongness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-based humor - shows Apu's intelligence and exasperation perfectly. Highly quotable line that works in many contexts

jokes.text: The Indian rope trick has become the Indian nope trick.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining his sexless marriage
jokes.punchline: Clever pun on 'rope trick' becoming 'nope trick' - no sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that's culturally specific to Apu, economical, and perfectly captures his sexual frustration. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, take a penny, leave a penny.
jokes.setup: Woman being flirty with Apu
jokes.punchline: She interprets the convenience store penny tray as flirtatious
jokes.explanation: Clever use of mundane convenience store element as innuendo - shows her flirty personality

jokes.text: Don't go Mary Todd on us.
jokes.setup: Someone getting impatient about Civil War reenactment
jokes.punchline: Reference to Mary Todd Lincoln's supposed mental instability
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference joke - turns Mary Todd Lincoln into a verb meaning 'go crazy'

jokes.text: One drink won't hurt... Hey, you're right. I was afraid it would start me drinking again, but it didn't!
jokes.setup: Barney worried about drinking because he's recovering alcoholic
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves he's wrong by having the drink anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic Barney - perfect character consistency showing his complete lack of willpower around alcohol

jokes.text: The second battle of Springfield was fought by the North, the South, and the East to keep Springfield in, out of, and next to the Union, respectively.
jokes.setup: Historical explanation of Civil War battle
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical multiple sides with contradictory goals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on Civil War complexity - 'next to the Union' is particularly ridiculous

jokes.text: Now, the actual battle was fought over there where that man is standing, but he won't move, so we'll do it here.
jokes.setup: Explaining historical battlefield location
jokes.punchline: Casual admission they're doing it wrong because of one stubborn guy
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery about bureaucratic compromise over historical accuracy - very relatable modern frustration

jokes.text: Bang, bang. Pow, pow. Hit with stick. Hit with stick. Die, you bastard.
jokes.setup: Civil War battle reenactment beginning
jokes.punchline: Increasingly crude and non-period battle sounds culminating in modern profanity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from childish sound effects to violence - shows amateurish nature of reenactment

jokes.text: For me, the war is over! This battlefield is rife with inaccuracy.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy participating in Civil War reenactment
jokes.punchline: He dramatically quits over historical inaccuracies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - his obsession with accuracy extends to historical reenactments. Very quotable.

jokes.text: You, dead people, stop playing cards! And Stonewall Jackson, stop rollerblading!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy listing historical inaccuracies
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous anachronisms - dead soldiers playing cards, Stonewall Jackson on rollerblades
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurd anachronisms - 'Stonewall Jackson rollerblading' is memorably ridiculous. Great visual comedy.

jokes.text: The South will boogie again!
jokes.setup: Stonewall Jackson on rollerblades
jokes.punchline: Confederate battle cry updated for disco era - 'boogie' instead of 'rise'
jokes.explanation: Clever pun on 'The South will rise again' - perfectly captures the anachronistic chaos of the reenactment

jokes.text: Truly, you are the greatest generation.
jokes.setup: Memorial Day speech honoring WWII veterans
jokes.punchline: Direct reference to Tom Brokaw's famous book title
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference - sets up the Brokaw callback but not particularly clever on its own

jokes.text: Keep it coming, Brokaw!
jokes.setup: Speaker using Tom Brokaw's famous phrase about WWII generation
jokes.punchline: Veteran directly addresses Brokaw as if he's there giving the speech
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows the veterans' awareness of their media coverage - breaking fourth wall slightly

jokes.text: You're damn right, it is! You can't thank us enough! Every generation stinks but ours!
jokes.setup: Veteran enjoying praise for his generation
jokes.punchline: Takes it too far by declaring every other generation terrible
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how praise can go to someone's head - captures real generational tensions

jokes.text: Not the pointed end, you dumbass!
jokes.setup: Civil War surrender scene
jokes.punchline: Modern profanity during supposedly historical moment about sword handling
jokes.explanation: Decent anachronism joke - modern language breaking through historical roleplay

jokes.text: Well, then you're definitely not going to like my steam-powered super spider. With the stepping and the squishing and the webs made of nylon.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy complaining about historical inaccuracy
jokes.punchline: Frink's completely anachronistic mechanical spider with modern materials
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frink moment - his weird inventions appearing in completely inappropriate contexts. 'Webs made of nylon' is wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: Why do you always take the side of local merchants?
jokes.setup: Marge suggests the keg might not get full deposit back
jokes.punchline: Homer's paranoid conspiracy theory about Marge favoring businesses
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's tendency toward bizarre conspiracy theories about completely innocent situations

jokes.text: Now, Apu, when you gave me this keg, it had dents. And here's proof.
jokes.setup: Homer returning damaged keg and claiming it was already dented
jokes.punchline: Shows 'proof' which is obviously freshly created evidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer scheme - terrible lie with 'evidence' that obviously doesn't prove his point. Visual element adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: That giggle is none of my business, or is it?
jokes.setup: Homer hears Apu and Squishee Lady giggling
jokes.punchline: Homer's internal conflict about whether to investigate
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the affair subplot - shows Homer's nosy nature and inner dialogue

jokes.text: Squishee Lady, you've had less than eight kids, haven't you? Haven't you?
jokes.setup: Homer investigating possible affair
jokes.punchline: Homer's interrogation technique focuses on this completely irrelevant detail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - focusing on the wrong details during his investigation. The repetition makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Please do the Hindu that you do so well.
jokes.setup: Apu and Squishee Lady in romantic situation
jokes.punchline: Play on 'voodoo that you do' but makes it about his ethnicity
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but relies on ethnic stereotyping - shows the affair's inappropriate nature

jokes.text: Nothing. Nothing. There's nothing eating me up inside.
jokes.setup: Marge asking what's wrong with Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's obvious lie delivered in the most suspicious way possible
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's terrible acting when trying to hide something. The repetition sells it.

jokes.text: Oh, I hope no one makes any double entendres.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about awkward badminton game with affair knowledge
jokes.punchline: Marge hoping to avoid exactly what the scene will be full of
jokes.explanation: Good setup line that promises the audience exactly what they'll get - dramatic irony at its finest

jokes.text: Oh, Apu, you keep scoring while my back is turned. Are you sure you're not cheating?
jokes.setup: Badminton game with affair subtext
jokes.punchline: Manjula unknowingly describes exactly what Apu is doing romantically
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Manjula's innocent badminton observation perfectly describes his affair. Economical and layered.

jokes.text: Are you sure you're not cheating?
jokes.setup: Continuing the badminton game conversation
jokes.punchline: Direct question about badminton that's really about the affair
jokes.explanation: Escalates the double entendre perfectly - most direct question possible with maximum double meaning

jokes.text: Dirty love. I mean 30-love! I mean, anyone for penis? Tennis!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to keep affair secret during badminton
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly inappropriate Freudian slips
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating Freudian slips - each one gets worse. 'Anyone for penis?' is perfectly Homer. Great comedic timing.

jokes.text: I'll just get the shuttlecock. Oh!
jokes.setup: Continued awkward badminton scene
jokes.punchline: Marge realizes she said another inadvertently sexual word
jokes.explanation: Decent continuation of the double entendre theme - Marge's innocent reaction to saying 'shuttlecock'

jokes.text: You're not in any physical pain, the only kind of pain a man can understand.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why Marge is crying
jokes.punchline: Homer's admission that men only understand physical pain
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational comedy about male emotional limitations - very quotable and universally relatable

jokes.text: What would that accomplish? That guy's hilarious. His reaction would be priceless.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting they tell Krusty about Apu's affair
jokes.punchline: Homer only wants to tell Krusty for entertainment value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - turning someone's marriage crisis into personal entertainment. Shows his priorities.

jokes.text: That's sad. All those kids. I think he's building to something.
jokes.setup: Homer tells Krusty about Apu's affair
jokes.punchline: Krusty's underwhelming reaction - he thinks it's setup to a joke
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - Krusty doesn't give the reaction Homer expected, treating real drama like a comedy setup

jokes.text: Let's say this pepper got married to this salt shaker, and along comes sexy Mrs. Dash.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain adultery to Apu using condiments
jokes.punchline: Absurd metaphor using salt, pepper, and Mrs. Dash seasoning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor - using condiments to explain adultery is perfectly Homer. 'Sexy Mrs. Dash' is memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: Watch and learn, you dinks.
jokes.setup: Barney offering to help with woman at bar
jokes.punchline: Barney, who's terrible with women, calling others 'dinks'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Barney's unwarranted confidence despite his obvious incompetence with women

jokes.text: I saw you and that Squishee Lady canoodling like junkyard rabbits!
jokes.setup: Homer confronting Apu about affair
jokes.punchline: Homer's colorful description combining 'canoodling' with 'junkyard rabbits'
jokes.explanation: Great Homer vocabulary - 'canoodling like junkyard rabbits' is both euphemistic and vivid. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Is that Windex? It's Windelle. I can't afford Windex.
jokes.setup: Moe serving tropical drinks
jokes.punchline: Revealing he uses knockoff window cleaner instead of real alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - cheap, unsafe bar practices with brand-specific details. 'Windelle' is a great fake brand name.

jokes.text: Whoa, ha-mahatama, hatama, hatama, hatama, hatama, hatama, hatama.
jokes.setup: Apu trying to resist Squishee Lady's advances
jokes.punchline: Apu making nonsensical sounds while being seduced
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy - Apu's verbal breakdown under temptation, though not particularly original

jokes.text: How can you even accuse me of repeated infidelity?
jokes.setup: Manjula suspicious about Apu's behavior
jokes.punchline: Apu's choice of words admits to multiple affairs while denying them
jokes.explanation: Excellent word choice comedy - 'repeated infidelity' accidentally admits guilt while trying to sound innocent

jokes.text: Oh, Apu, scratch and win! Mmm! That's good adultery!
jokes.setup: Surveillance camera footage of the affair
jokes.punchline: Squishee Lady treating affair like a lottery ticket and Apu rating it
jokes.explanation: Creative combination of lottery ticket language with adultery - 'That's good adultery!' like a food commercial is memorable

jokes.text: Caramel baloney.
jokes.setup: Apu saying he thought karma was baloney
jokes.punchline: Mishearing/mispronouncing as 'caramel baloney'
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific malapropism - shows Apu's occasional language confusion in a food-related way

jokes.text: If you don't like losing at cribbage, stay out of my place.
jokes.setup: Kirk talking to homeless Apu
jokes.punchline: Kirk's pathetically aggressive attitude about board games
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kirk moment - taking board games way too seriously, showing his desperate need for control

jokes.text: No, no! I'll let you win. I'll let you win. God, I'm really lonely.
jokes.setup: Kirk being aggressive about cribbage
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal showing his desperation for companionship
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Kirk's pathetic reversal from tough to desperate in seconds, very relatable sadness

jokes.text: It's a pleasure to eat your lead, good sir.
jokes.setup: McBain in action scene
jokes.punchline: Overly polite phrasing for violent action movie dialogue
jokes.explanation: Good McBain parody - mixing extreme politeness with violence, typical of his character's Austrian formality

jokes.text: You might hear from your friends or co-workers that Apu isn't living at home anymore.
jokes.setup: Talking to kids about Apu's situation
jokes.punchline: Suggesting kids have friends and co-workers like adults
jokes.explanation: Decent joke about treating kids like adults - 'co-workers' is the funny detail that doesn't fit

jokes.text: You can run, but you can't glide!
jokes.setup: Homer's fantasy sequence
jokes.punchline: Variation on 'you can run but you can't hide' with flying context
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on familiar phrase - works well in the flying context of Homer's fantasy

jokes.text: Is it my husband's whore?
jokes.setup: Marge saying they have a special guest
jokes.punchline: Manjula's bitter, direct question about the affair
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Manjula's blunt response cuts through the social pleasantries perfectly. Very economical.

jokes.text: I order you to get back together or I'll suck your blood! Blah, blah!
jokes.setup: Bart dressed as Hindu god trying to help marriage
jokes.punchline: Mixing Hindu deity with vampire threats
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor - Bart mixing religious authority with vampire cliches, though somewhat random

jokes.text: Or should I say blandoori?
jokes.setup: Manjula criticizing store-bought tandoori
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'bland' with 'tandoori'
jokes.explanation: Good food pun showing Manjula's cooking standards and wit even while angry

jokes.text: These are divorce papers! Consider yourself served!
jokes.setup: Romantic reconciliation scene building
jokes.punchline: Manjula reveals she's serving divorce papers instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience expects reconciliation but gets the opposite. Well-structured reversal.

jokes.text: Can I lick your fingers? Meet me at the mail slot.
jokes.setup: Delivery person bringing fudge
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate request and bizarre meeting location
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor - weird delivery guy with inappropriate requests adds to the chaos

jokes.text: Because legally, I am allowed to shake him by the ankles and see what falls out. It's established in the case of Lawyers v. Justice.
jokes.setup: Lawyer asking if Apu has money
jokes.punchline: Fake legal precedent allowing cartoonish debt collection
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist legal humor - 'Lawyers v. Justice' is brilliant case name suggesting lawyers won against justice itself

jokes.text: No offense, but you remind me of the monkey man who slaughtered my family's chickens.
jokes.setup: Manjula talking to lawyer
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and dark comparison delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the casual delivery of such a specific, violent backstory is perfectly absurd and memorable

jokes.text: When will you humans learn that your feelings, as you call them, can stand in the way of big cash payoffs?
jokes.setup: Lawyer frustrated by emotional concerns
jokes.punchline: Dehumanizing clients while dismissing emotions for money
jokes.explanation: Great lawyer stereotype - treating clients as different species while prioritizing money over emotions

jokes.text: Mommy... Will... You... Let... Daddy... Come... Back? Cookie!
jokes.setup: Octuplets saying their first words
jokes.punchline: Heartfelt plea for family reunion ending with random 'Cookie!'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - builds emotional moment then undercuts with typical toddler priorities

jokes.text: All right! Girls night out! Exotic male dancers at the Golden Banana!
jokes.setup: Manjula wanting payback against Apu
jokes.punchline: Marge's enthusiastic suggestion of strip club
jokes.explanation: Great character subversion - wholesome Marge eagerly suggesting male strippers

jokes.text: Then I shred the list and put it in his food.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining how she handles Homer's wrongdoing
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive revenge involving literal paper consumption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Marge moment - reveals her passive-aggressive side with a genuinely disturbing revenge method

jokes.text: First I was a tiger, a snake, a clod, a goat with a hat, then me. A tapeworm, then assistant to Lorne Michaels!
jokes.setup: Apu contemplating reincarnation
jokes.punchline: Series ending with 'assistant to Lorne Michaels' as ultimate punishment
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - builds through animal forms to the ultimate punishment of working for difficult SNL producer

jokes.text: First I was a tiger, a snake, a clod, a goat with a hat, then me. A tapeworm, then assistant to Lorne Michaels!
jokes.setup: Apu contemplating reincarnation cycle
jokes.punchline: Working for Lorne Michaels as worse fate than being tapeworm
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and showbiz satire - being Lorne Michaels' assistant worse than tapeworm

jokes.text: Number four, legally change name to Slime Q. Slimedog. Number five, wear nametag that says same.
jokes.setup: Manjula's list of tasks for Apu
jokes.punchline: Increasingly humiliating demands culminating in ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - 'Slime Q. Slimedog' is memorably ridiculous, and requiring the nametag makes it worse

jokes.text: My Smooshie tastes like a shopping bag. Mine tastes like dog fur!
jokes.setup: Apu changed from Squishees to Smooshies
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disgusting flavor descriptions
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of disgusting flavors - shows consequences of Apu's guilt-driven business changes

jokes.text: He is hideous. Oh, a challenge!
jokes.setup: Noting the ugly deliveryman
jokes.punchline: Selma's romantic desperation turning ugliness into attraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selma characterization - her dating desperation turning flaws into features

jokes.text: I bought the issue for Richard Avedon's pictures of Lenny.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy admiring Apu's New Yorker cartoon
jokes.punchline: Pretentious reason for buying magazine (famous photographer's pictures of Lenny)
jokes.explanation: Good Comic Book Guy pretentiousness - highbrow photographer mixed with Simpsons character

jokes.text: I liked all the roles filled by minority actors. Why, I didn't even notice.
jokes.setup: Octuplets performing My Fair Lady
jokes.punchline: Claiming not to notice race while specifically mentioning it
jokes.explanation: Sharp social satire about performative racial awareness - perfectly captures liberal hypocrisy

jokes.text: Thank you very much, you big fat blabbermouth! I... Sorry, sorry. It's been a rough month.
jokes.setup: Homer being thanked
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate angry response followed by weak excuse
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - inappropriate anger followed by pathetic excuse shows his emotional instability

jokes.text: Don't worry, I soaked it in the toilet to soften it up.
jokes.setup: Homer helping Apu eat a lightbulb
jokes.punchline: Reveals disgusting preparation method intended as helpful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - genuinely trying to help but making things infinitely worse

jokes.text: Don't worry, I soaked it in the toilet to soften it up.
jokes.setup: Homer giving Apu a lightbulb to eat
jokes.punchline: Disgusting preparation method for making lightbulb 'edible'
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out Homer logic - trying to be helpful in completely wrong way

tmdb_overview: Homer and Marge discover that Apu is having an affair with the Squishee delivery lady at the Kwik-E-Mart. They decide to keep Apu's wife Manjula from finding out about it. However, she eventually learns of Apu's affair by watching store security tapes. She throws Apu out of the house and decides to file for divorce, but soon realizes that she misses him.
